games.credit: Sarah Hill, Jordan Crofts, Alex Milton, Kyle Rampal, Moinuddin Sami
games.author: Sarah Hill
games.title: Seconds To Spare
games.interview: Seconds to Spare was a year long project that ran over two consecutive semesters at Macquarie University in 2025. It was the first opportunity that I was given to work with a chosen team and make a video game from start to finish, and the original concept for the game was actually one I had created years prior. Being able to see that idea come to fruition was incredible, and the feeling I had when it was finally finished was something that really cemented the idea that choosing game design and development was the right choice. /n There was a lot of hesitation when I was deciding what kind of career I wanted to chase out of high school, and I had to weigh up whether I could feasibly chase a creative career and do what I really wanted to do, in an artistic industry that can be unforgiving. But taking that chance has been worth it; game development has provided me a way to express myself so loudly in my very own little corner of the internet, and having my first ever tangible result of my hard work was, and still is, indescribable.

games.credit: Evelyn Greener, Mira Huhta, Rabi Attaii, Aleyna Baran
games.author: Evelyn Greener
games.title: Hoppyscotch
games.interview: Games have been there for us in many of our darkest moments. I came to game development with the desire to create a game that could be there for at least one person the way games were there for me. Game development is a craft I've fallen in love with, and I wish for all of us to have our voices heard, especially those spoken over.
games.description: Hoppyscotch is a 2D puzzle game about frogs and equity, where you create accessible paths to get all frogs get home. Sometimes someone needs an extra hand, and that's okay!

games.credit: Elswyth Porter, Georgina Moujalli, Harley Doolan, Hugh Guest, Jacques Roberts, Kerry Lin, Kurt Tikoft, Mungo Lennon, Ryan Crowe, Xavier Heta Cleret
games.author: Kurt Tikoft
games.title: VULTUR
games.interview: VULTUR's origins lie in a personal interest I have in the concepts of presence, friction, and fantasy in game design. It was inspired by the 'mech-shooter' subgenre exemplified by the MechWarrior and GunGriffon games, as well as the evocative aesthetic of bio-punk artwork. These come together as a game about manipulating a bio-mech, a lumbering machine capable of extreme destructive power, with a weight that is felt in every action the player takes. As they defeat enemies, players can rip their own weapons off the bio-mech, replacing them with the weapons of defeated enemies like a carrion feeder. From my original vision, I think everyone on the team took away something slightly different. Some team members were most interested in the core shooting mechanics and atmosphere, while others were more interested in deep buildcrafting and narrative. The potpourri of perspectives was beneficial in the iterative development stage, resulting in mechanics that push the boundaries of action gameplay. However, as the project progressed, this resulted in a lack of consensus and clarity, and the game began to fracture at the seams. With hindsight, I wish we addressed this earlier, but at the same time I'm glad we took a creative risk. In a time where studios and audiences seem to be ossifying along genre lines, I hope we as developers can keep challenging what a game can be.
games.description: VULTUR is an atmospheric Mech FPS with Action RPG build creation. /n Craft unique builds with different weapons and modular upgrades as you explore and scavenge what you can off of defeated enemies. /n Delve into a foggy, grotesque alien wasteland, left barren and hostile after ‘superior’ beings made the planet their personal test bed for all manner of horrifying, hubris-fulfilling science experiments. From their intervention, only biologically warped creatures that survived or were created now walk the surface hunting each other for meat and parts. /n Taking the role of a nervous system infused into a biomechanical husk; set out to search the barren wasteland, fighting other biomechs, tearing weapons and unique upgrades straight out of their flesh. Then, combine weapons and modules to create intricate, powerful combinations that tear through enemies. /n Death will reset you back to a nil build, allowing you to attempt new runs with different weapon builds in the next life. /n Holding TAB to access your weapons menu will allow you to pick up dropped weapons and passive modules off enemy mechs and turrets as well as tear your own weapons and modules off your body for complete customisability.

games.credit: Credit: Anton A, Isaac James, Super McTea, xen-oli-th
games.author: Team
games.title: Personal Space
games.interview: At L Stands for Winner we're passionate about creating games because they're a versatile medium that can bring joy, tell stories, and create interesting experiences. We also believe the game development process to be complex but rewarding. It's the intersection of so many creative and academic disciplines—the psychology of engaging the player, the math of physics and graphics programming, 3D modelling and 2D art, music composition and sound design—each integral.
games.description: Personal Space is an important project to us because, at the culmination of our studies, it was an opportunity to bring our acquired expertise together to create a polished game. Each of us contributed something unique; procedural generation for the map, the spaceship models and the flocking algorithm to move them, the composition of the soundtrack, homegrown shaders for the planets and sun, and more. It's because of our diverse skillsets that we could bring it to fruition.
